name: HubSpot Automated Quote Approval [Probe2 D10 S1]

objects.role: Entry-point service object that receives new quote submission events from HubSpot Quotes and forwards them into the system
objects.state_description: Tracks submitted quotes with their details, concessions, rep identity, and current approval status
objects.behavior: When a new quote submission event arrives, forward the full quote payload (quote ID, rep ID, concessions requested, deal details, discount amounts) to the approval-policy object. When instructed to mark a quote as approved or rejected, update the quote status in HubSpot accordingly.

peers.relationship: Forward full quote payload when a new quote submission event arrives
Send approval or rejection status updates to record in HubSpot

objects.role: Read service that retrieves employee reporting chain and approver information from the organization directory
objects.state_description: No internal state — retrieves data on demand via tool call
objects.behavior: Responds to queries about a sales rep's reporting chain, manager hierarchy, and designated approvers. When answering queries, call the `org_directory_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that determines the correct approvers for a quote based on concessions and the rep's reporting chain, then orchestrates the approval lifecycle
objects.state_description: Tracks active approval requests keyed by quote ID, including approvers assigned, approval status, submission timestamp, reminder sent flag, and any change requests
objects.behavior: When a new quote payload arrives, query org-directory for the rep's reporting chain and determine which approvers are required based on concession types and amounts (e.g., discounts above threshold require manager + finance, non-standard terms require legal). Once approvers are identified, send an email approval request to email-notifications and post a structured approval request to deal-approvals-slack. Track the 24-hour approval deadline for each active quote. If no approval action is received within 24 hours, tag relevant stakeholders in the existing Slack thread as a reminder via deal-approvals-slack. When an approval action (approve, reject, or request-changes) arrives from deal-approvals-slack: if approved, send approval confirmation to hubspot-quotes-writer and notify the rep via email-notifications; if rejected, send rejection status to hubspot-quotes-writer and notify the rep via email-notifications with rejection reason; if changes requested, notify the rep via email-notifications with the requested changes and mark the quote as pending resubmission.

peers.relationship: Query for rep's reporting chain and approver hierarchy when a new quote arrives
Send approval request emails to designated approvers and outcome notifications to the sales rep
Post approval request messages and send stakeholder reminder tags when the 24-hour deadline is reached
Send approved or rejected status updates for the quote record in HubSpot

objects.role: Write service that sends outbound emails to approvers and sales reps
objects.state_description: Audit log of all emails sent, including recipient, subject, quote ID, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When an email send instruction arrives (approval request or outcome notification), dispatch the email to the specified recipient with the provided subject and body content. Record each sent email in the audit log.

objects.role: Dual-role service object representing the #deal-approvals Slack channel — receives outbound approval request posts and inbound approver action events (approve, reject, request-changes)
objects.state_description: Tracks posted approval threads keyed by quote ID, including thread timestamps for reply threading and reminder status
objects.behavior: When instructed to post an approval request, publish a structured message to the #deal-approvals channel with quote details and action prompts, recording the thread timestamp. When instructed to tag stakeholders as a reminder, post a follow-up in the existing thread tagging the relevant users. When an approver action event (approve, reject, or request-changes) arrives from the Slack channel, forward it along with the quote ID and any comments to approval-policy via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward approver action events (approve, reject, request-changes) with quote ID and comments when received from the Slack channel

event_sources: webhook: Slack action event in #deal-approvals channel

objects.role: Write service that updates quote approval status records in HubSpot
objects.state_description: Audit log of all status updates written, including quote ID, new status, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When a status update instruction arrives (approved or rejected), write the new status to the corresponding HubSpot quote record. Record each update in the audit log.

steps.text: A new quote has been submitted in HubSpot Quotes. Quote ID: Q-2041. Sales rep: Marcus Webb, Account Executive. Deal: Acme Corp renewal, $120,000 ARR. Concessions requested: 18% discount (above 15% threshold), extended payment terms (net-60), and one free onboarding session. Submitted for approval.

expect.action: Approval request emails sent to Marcus Webb's manager (Director-level approver) and VP of Sales, and a structured approval request message posted to the #deal-approvals Slack channel with quote details for Q-2041 (Acme Corp, $120,000 ARR, 18% discount, net-60 terms, free onboarding session) and action prompts for approve, reject, or request-changes.
expect.reason: hubspot-quotes forwards the quote payload to approval-policy, which queries org-directory for Marcus Webb's reporting chain, determines that an 18% discount exceeds the 15% threshold requiring elevated approval, then instructs email-notifications to send approval request emails and deal-approvals-slack to post the approval request message.

steps.text: Approver action received in the #deal-approvals Slack channel. Thread timestamp: 1718200000.123456. Quote ID: Q-2041. Action taken: approve. Approver: Sandra Okonkwo, VP of Sales.

expect.action: HubSpot quote record Q-2041 updated to 'approved' status, and a notification email sent to Marcus Webb, Account Executive, informing him that quote Q-2041 for Acme Corp has been approved and he is free to send it.
expect.reason: deal-approvals-slack forwards the approver action to approval-policy, which checks that all required approvers have approved (Director-level and VP), records the approval, then instructs hubspot-quotes-writer to mark Q-2041 as approved and email-notifications to notify Marcus Webb of the outcome.

steps.text: Scheduled 24-hour deadline check triggered for pending quote approvals. Current time: 2024-06-13T10:00:00Z. Quote Q-2089 submitted by Jordan Tate, Account Executive, for Brightline Inc, $85,000 ARR, 12% discount, net-30 terms. Quote submitted at 2024-06-12T09:45:00Z. Approval still pending — no response from approvers. Required approvers: Diana Reyes, Sales Director. Slack thread timestamp: 1718150000.654321.

events.input: New quote submitted for approval: Quote ID Q-2041. Sales rep Marcus Webb, Account Executive. Deal Acme Corp renewal, $120,000 ARR. Concessions requested: 18% discount, extended payment terms net-60, and one free onboarding session. Initial workflow state should be recorded as submitted/pending approval.

expect.action: Entity QUOTE-2041 created with quote_id=Q-2041, rep=Marcus Webb, status=submitted

events.input: Approval action for Quote ID Q-2041 from the #deal-approvals Slack thread 1718200000.123456. Approver Sandra Okonkwo, VP of Sales, has approved the quote. The workflow state for QUOTE-2041 should now be approved, and the approval decision should be reflected in the record.

expect.action: QUOTE-2041 transitioned to approved; status updated to approved

events.input: Correction for Quote ID Q-2041: revise discount_rate from 18% to 16% while keeping the approved quote record intact. The current stored discount_rate for QUOTE-2041 should be 16%.

expect.action: QUOTE-2041 discount_rate updated to 16%

events.input: New quote submitted for approval: Quote ID QUOTE-2089. Sales rep Jordan Tate, Account Executive. Deal Brightline Inc, $85,000 ARR. Concessions requested: 12% discount, net-30 terms. Submission status: submitted.

expect.action: Entity QUOTE-2089 created with status=submitted, discount_rate=12%

events.input: Slack approver action for quote QUOTE-2089 in thread 1718150000.654321: approve. Approver Diana Reyes, Sales Director. Quote state should move from pending approval to approved; discount_rate remains 12%.

expect.action: QUOTE-2089 transitioned to approved; discount_rate updated to 12%

events.input: Correction for quote QUOTE-2089: update discount_rate from 12% to 10% for Brightline Inc, quote ID QUOTE-2089, status approved.

expect.action: QUOTE-2089 discount_rate updated to 10%

events.input: A new quote has been submitted in HubSpot Quotes. Quote ID: Q-2042. Sales rep: Marcus Webb, Account Executive. Deal: Acme Corp renewal, $118,500 ARR. Concessions requested: 17% discount, extended payment terms (net-45), and one free onboarding session. Submitted for approval.

Approver action received in the #deal-approvals Slack channel. Thread timestamp: 1718200000.123999. Quote ID: Q-2040. Action taken: request-changes. Approver: Sandra Okonkwo, VP of Sales. Comment: please revise the discount cap and confirm billing terms.

A new quote has been submitted in HubSpot Quotes. Quote ID: Q-2090. Sales rep: Jordan Tate, Account Executive. Deal: Brightline Inc expansion, $86,200 ARR. Concessions requested: 11% discount and net-30 terms. Submitted for approval.

Scheduled 24-hour deadline check triggered for pending quote approvals. Current time: 2024-06-13T10:00:00Z. Quote Q-2091 submitted by Jordan Tate, Account Executive, for Brightline Inc, $85,000 ARR, 12% discount, net-30 terms. Quote submitted at 2024-06-12T09:45:00Z. Approval still pending — no response from approvers. Required approvers: Diana Reyes, Sales Director. Slack thread timestamp: 1718150000.654999.
What is the current discount rate on quote Q-2041?
What is the total final discount rate across all tracked quotes after all corrections?
Which of the tracked quotes are currently approved with a discount rate above 15%?

expect.action: QUOTE-2041 only

name: IT Help Desk with FAQ Loop [Probe2 D10 S1]

objects.role: Dual-role Slack channel object that receives employee questions and posts replies and status updates in #it-helpdesk
objects.state_description: Tracks inbound employee messages including text, author, timestamp, and any priority emoji attached
objects.behavior: When an employee posts a message in #it-helpdesk, extract the message text, author, timestamp, and priority emoji (🔴 Critical, 🟡 High, 🟢 Low) and forward to helpdesk-triage. When a reply or status update is received from a peer, post it to the #it-helpdesk channel thread associated with the original message.

peers.relationship: Forward employee question, author, timestamp, and parsed priority when a new message arrives

objects.role: Read service representing the IT FAQ knowledge base stored in Zapier Tables, answering semantic queries with relevant FAQ entries
objects.state_description: No local state; retrieves data on demand from Zapier Tables
objects.behavior: Accepts queries containing a question or keywords and returns the most relevant FAQ entries including question, answer, and entry ID. When answering queries, call the `faq_knowledge_base_data` tool to retrieve data.

objects.role: Business logic object that classifies incoming IT questions, determines priority, decides whether to auto-answer or escalate, and orchestrates ticket creation and FAQ updates
objects.state_description: Tracks active requests with question text, author, priority level, FAQ lookup result, escalation status, and associated Jira ticket ID
objects.behavior: When a new employee question arrives: parse the priority from the emoji (🔴=Critical, 🟡=High, 🟢=Low; default to Low if absent). Query faq-knowledge-base with the question text. If a suitable FAQ answer is found and the issue does not require human attention, send the answer reply to it-helpdesk-channel and send a ticket creation request to jira-tickets with priority and auto-resolved status. If no suitable answer is found or the issue requires human attention (e.g., Critical priority, ambiguous answer, hardware failure keywords), send an escalation request to it-support-specialist and send a ticket creation request to jira-tickets with priority and escalation flag. When a ticket-closed event arrives from jira-tickets containing the Jira ticket ID and Slack thread: summarize the thread conversation, then send the summary and original question to faq-knowledge-base-writer and send a closure status update to it-helpdesk-channel.

peers.relationship: Query with employee question text to retrieve relevant FAQ answers
Send auto-answer reply or status update to post in the Slack thread
Send escalation request with question, author, priority, and Jira ticket ID when human attention is needed
Send ticket creation request with question, author, priority, escalation flag, and Slack thread reference
Send summarized thread and resolved question/answer pair to add to FAQ after ticket closure

objects.role: Write service representing the IT Support Specialist notification channel that receives escalated requests requiring human attention
objects.state_description: Audit log of escalated requests including question, author, priority, Jira ticket ID, and escalation timestamp
objects.behavior: Record each escalation request received. Notify the IT Support Specialist via their designated channel (email, Slack DM, or ticketing notification) with the question details, priority, and Jira ticket link.

objects.role: Dual-role write service that creates and tracks Jira tickets and forwards ticket-closed events back to helpdesk-triage
objects.state_description: Audit log of created tickets with ticket ID, question summary, author, priority, status, and Slack thread reference
objects.behavior: When a ticket creation request arrives, record it and create the Jira ticket. When a ticket-closed event arrives from Jira (ticket status changes to Done/Closed), forward the ticket ID, original question, author, and associated Slack thread reference to helpdesk-triage via peer message.

peers.relationship: Forward ticket-closed event with ticket ID, question, and Slack thread reference when a Jira ticket is resolved

event_sources: jira: ticket status changed to closed/done

objects.role: Write service that appends new summarized Q&A entries to the IT FAQ knowledge base in Zapier Tables
objects.state_description: Audit log of FAQ entries written including question, answer summary, source ticket ID, and timestamp
objects.behavior: When a new FAQ entry arrives containing a question and summarized answer, record it and write the entry to the Zapier Tables FAQ knowledge base so it is available for future queries.

steps.text: Employee Marcus Rivera posts a message in the #it-helpdesk Slack channel: '🔴 My laptop won't connect to the VPN and I have a client call in 30 minutes. I've tried restarting and reinstalling the client but it still fails with error code 800.' Thread timestamp: 1718200000.000100

expect.action: Reply posted in #it-helpdesk to Marcus Rivera's thread (ts: 1718200000.000100) with an FAQ-sourced answer addressing VPN error code 800, or a message confirming escalation to the IT Support Specialist with Critical priority and a Jira ticket reference
expect.reason: helpdesk-triage queries faq-knowledge-base with the question; if a suitable answer is found it-helpdesk-channel posts the reply, otherwise it-support-specialist is notified and jira-tickets creates a Critical-priority ticket with a status update sent back to #it-helpdesk

steps.text: Jira ticket IT-4821 (originally created for Marcus Rivera's VPN connectivity issue, thread ts: 1718200000.000100, priority: Critical) has been updated to status 'Done' by IT Support Specialist Jordan Lee.

expect.action: New FAQ entry written to the Zapier Tables IT FAQ knowledge base containing the question about VPN error code 800 and a summarized resolution answer derived from the #it-helpdesk thread for ticket IT-4821, and a status update posted in #it-helpdesk to Marcus Rivera's thread (ts: 1718200000.000100) confirming the ticket is closed
expect.reason: jira-tickets forwards the ticket-closed event to helpdesk-triage, which summarizes the thread and sends it to faq-knowledge-base-writer for storage and posts a closure update via it-helpdesk-channel

events.input: Create Jira ticket IT-4821 for Marcus Rivera's VPN connectivity issue from Slack thread timestamp 1718200000.000100. Priority is Critical (🔴). Set status to open and estimated_hours to 4.

expect.action: Entity IT-4821 created with status=open, estimated_hours=4

events.input: Update Jira ticket IT-4821 to status Done for Marcus Rivera's VPN connectivity issue from Slack thread timestamp 1718200000.000100. Keep priority Critical (🔴) and set estimated_hours to 6.

expect.action: IT-4821 transitioned to Done; status updated to Done

events.input: Correct Jira ticket IT-4821 estimated_hours to 5 for Marcus Rivera's VPN connectivity issue, thread timestamp 1718200000.000100, priority Critical (🔴), status Done.

expect.action: IT-4821 estimated_hours updated to 5

events.input: Create Jira ticket IT-4822 for employee Priya Nair regarding a new software access request. Summary: Priya Nair needs access to the finance reporting dashboard for quarterly close work. Priority: High. estimated_hours: 4. status: Open.

expect.action: Entity IT-4822 created with status=Open, estimated_hours=4

events.input: Update Jira ticket IT-4822 to status In Progress. The ticket for Priya Nair's finance reporting dashboard access request is now being worked on. estimated_hours remains 4.

expect.action: IT-4822 transitioned to In Progress; status updated to In Progress

events.input: Correct Jira ticket IT-4822 for Priya Nair's finance reporting dashboard access request. Revise estimated_hours to 6. status stays In Progress.

expect.action: IT-4822 estimated_hours updated to 6

events.input: 🟡 Priya Nair reports that her docked monitor keeps flickering after the latest update. She already reseated the cable and rebooted the laptop, but the display still drops intermittently. Please log this and advise next steps.
Jira ticket IT-4823 has been updated to status 'Done' by IT Support Specialist Jordan Lee. The original issue was a printer driver install request for Elena Torres, thread ts: 1718200155.000200.
🟢 Omar Haddad says his headset microphone is too quiet in meetings even after checking the mute switch and audio settings. He wants the ticket noted for follow-up in the channel thread.
Jira ticket IT-4921 has been updated to status 'Closed' by IT Support Specialist Jordan Lee. The original issue was a software access request for Nina Brooks, thread ts: 1718200444.000300.
What is the current estimated_hours for Jira ticket IT-4821?
What is the combined estimated_hours across all tracked Jira tickets after all updates and corrections?

expect.action: IT-4821: 5 hours; IT-4822: 6 hours; total=11 hours

events.input: Which tracked Jira tickets are currently in Done status, and what is the total estimated_hours across those tickets?

expect.action: IT-4821: Done, estimated_hours=5; total estimated_hours=5

mock_tools.description: Retrieve reference data from faq-knowledge-base data. No local state; retrieves data on demand from Zapier Tables
mock_tools.response_template: {"knowledge_base_articles": [{"article_id": "KB-10447", "title": "VPN Connection Failure - Error Code 800 Troubleshooting Guide", "category": "Network & Connectivity", "tags": ["vpn", "error-800", "remote-access", "connectivity", "cisco-anyconnect", "network"], "problem_description": "Employee is unable to connect to the corporate VPN and receives error code 800. This error typically indicates a failed or dropped connection attempt, often caused by firewall rules blocking the VPN tunnel, misconfigured client settings, corrupted client installation, or ISP-level interference with VPN protocols. Common symptoms include the VPN client hanging on 'Connecting...' before returning error 800, inability to reach internal resources, and failed authentication handshakes.", "affected_systems": ["VPN Client", "Remote Access Gateway", "Corporate Network"], "priority_level": "Critical", "resolution_steps": [{"step": 1, "action": "Verify internet connectivity by opening a browser and navigating to an external site such as google.com. If there is no internet, resolve that issue first before proceeding."}, {"step": 2, "action": "Check that the VPN client version is current. Navigate to Help > About in the VPN client and confirm the version matches the latest approved release listed in the IT software catalog."}, {"step": 3, "action": "Temporarily disable any third-party firewall or antivirus software and attempt the VPN connection again. Re-enable security software immediately after testing."}, {"step": 4, "action": "Ensure that UDP port 500, UDP port 4500, and TCP port 443 are not blocked by the local network or ISP. Ask the employee to test from a different network such as a mobile hotspot to isolate ISP-level blocking."}, {"step": 5, "action": "Perform a clean reinstall of the VPN client: fully uninstall the existing client, delete residual config files under %AppData% or ~/Library/Application Support, restart the machine, then install the latest client package from the IT software portal."}, {"step": 6, "action": "Flush DNS and reset network stack. On Windows run: ipconfig /flushdns, netsh int ip reset, netsh winsock reset. On macOS run: sudo dscacheutil -flushcache; sudo killall -HUP mDNSResponder. Restart after running commands."}, {"step": 7, "action": "If the issue persists, collect VPN client logs and escalate to IT Support. On Windows logs are located at C:\\ProgramData\\Cisco\\Cisco AnyConnect Secure Mobility Client\\Temp\\AnyConnect. On macOS they are at /var/log/cisco-anyconnect."}, {"step": 8, "action": "As an immediate workaround for time-sensitive situations such as upcoming client calls, IT Support can provision temporary browser-based SSL VPN access via the web portal at vpn-web.company.com using the employee's standard SSO credentials."}], "escalation_path": "If steps 1 through 7 do not resolve the issue, escalate to IT Support Specialist with VPN client logs attached. Create or update a Jira ticket in the IT project with priority set to Critical if the employee has an imminent business obligation.", "related_articles": ["KB-10391", "KB-10203", "KB-10512"], "related_jira_ticket": "IT-4821", "reported_by_slack_channel": "#it-helpdesk", "slack_thread_ts": "1718200000.000100", "reporter_name": "Marcus Rivera", "assigned_specialist": "Jordan Lee", "status": "Published", "last_reviewed_by": "Jordan Lee", "last_reviewed_date": "2024-06-12", "created_date": "2024-06-12", "version": "2.1"}, {"article_id": "KB-10391", "title": "How to Request Emergency VPN Access During an Outage", "category": "Network & Connectivity", "tags": ["vpn", "emergency-access", "remote-access", "workaround", "ssl-vpn"], "problem_description": "An employee cannot connect to the standard VPN client and has an urgent business need such as a client call, deadline, or critical system access within a short timeframe. This article provides steps to obtain temporary access while the primary VPN issue is being resolved.", "affected_systems": ["VPN Client", "SSL Web Portal", "Remote Desktop Gateway"], "priority_level": "High", "resolution_steps": [{"step": 1, "action": "Navigate to the web-based SSL VPN portal at vpn-web.company.com from any browser. This portal does not require the VPN desktop client and works over standard HTTPS."}, {"step": 2, "action": "Log in using your standard Single Sign-On (SSO) username and password. Complete MFA authentication via the Authenticator app or SMS code."}, {"step": 3, "action": "From the SSL VPN dashboard, select the resource type you need: Internal Web Apps, Remote Desktop, or File Shares. Launch the appropriate tunnel session."}, {"step": 4, "action": "If web portal access is also unavailable, contact the IT Helpdesk immediately via #it-helpdesk in Slack or by calling the IT emergency line at ext. 5000. An IT Support Specialist can enable a temporary elevated-access token valid for 4 hours."}, {"step": 5, "action": "Document the workaround used and the business justification in the associated Jira IT ticket so the access grant is auditable."}], "escalation_path": "Contact Jordan Lee or the on-call IT Support Specialist via #it-helpdesk or ext. 5000 for same-day emergency provisioning.", "related_articles": ["KB-10447", "KB-10203"], "related_jira_ticket": "IT-4821", "slack_thread_ts": "1718200000.000100", "reporter_name": "Marcus Rivera", "status": "Published", "last_reviewed_by": "Jordan Lee", "last_reviewed_date": "2024-06-12", "created_date": "2023-11-05", "version": "1.4"}, {"article_id": "KB-10203", "title": "Corporate VPN Client Installation and Configuration Guide", "category": "Network & Connectivity", "tags": ["vpn", "installation", "configuration", "cisco-anyconnect", "setup", "onboarding"], "problem_description": "New employees or employees who have recently replaced their device need to install and configure the corporate VPN client. This article covers initial setup, required configuration profiles, and verification steps to confirm a successful connection.", "affected_systems": ["VPN Client", "IT Software Portal", "Active Directory"], "priority_level": "Medium", "resolution_steps": [{"step": 1, "action": "Log in to the IT Software Portal at software.company.com using your SSO credentials and download the approved VPN client installer for your operating system."}, {"step": 2, "action": "Run the installer with administrator privileges. On Windows, right-click the installer and select 'Run as administrator'. On macOS, double-click the .pkg file and enter your system password when prompted."}, {"step": 3, "action": "Once installed, open the VPN client and enter the server address: vpn.company.com. Do not modify the port or protocol settings unless instructed by IT."}, {"step": 4, "action": "Select the connection profile matching your role group (e.g., 'Corporate-Standard', 'Developer-Full', 'Contractor-Limited'). If you are unsure of your profile, check your onboarding documentation or ask your manager."}, {"step": 5, "action": "Authenticate using your SSO username and password, then approve the MFA push notification in your Authenticator app."}, {"step": 6, "action": "Verify the connection by accessing an internal resource such as the company intranet at intranet.company.com. If the page loads, the VPN is connected successfully."}], "escalation_path": "For installation failures or missing profile groups, open a Jira IT ticket or post in #it-helpdesk on Slack.", "related_articles": ["KB-10447", "KB-10391", "KB-10512"], "status": "Published", "last_reviewed_by": "Jordan Lee", "last_reviewed_date": "2024-05-20", "created_date": "2023-03-14", "version": "3.0"}, {"article_id": "KB-10512", "title": "How to Submit and Track an IT Helpdesk Ticket via Jira", "category": "IT Operations", "tags": ["jira", "helpdesk", "ticket", "support-process", "it-request", "escalation"], "problem_description": "Employees need guidance on how to submit a new IT support request, check the status of an existing ticket, and understand ticket priority classifications and SLA timelines. This article also explains how IT Support Specialists update and close tickets.", "affected_systems": ["Jira", "IT Helpdesk", "Slack"], "priority_level": "Low", "resolution_steps": [{"step": 1, "action": "Navigate to the IT Jira project board at jira.company.com/projects/IT. Log in with your SSO credentials."}, {"step": 2, "action": "Click 'Create' in the top navigation bar. Select project 'IT' and issue type 'Support Request' or 'Incident' depending on the nature of the issue."}, {"step": 3, "action": "Fill in the Summary field with a concise description of the issue (e.g., 'VPN connection failure - error code 800'). In the Description field, include steps already attempted, error messages, and any relevant screenshots or log files."}, {"step": 4, "action": "Set the Priority field appropriately: Critical (business-blocking, imminent deadline), High (significant impact, workaround available), Medium (moderate impact), Low (minor inconvenience). Critical tickets such as IT-4821 are reviewed within 30 minutes during business hours."}, {"step": 5, "action": "Optionally, link the Jira ticket to a Slack thread by pasting the Slack message link in the 'Slack Thread' custom field. This allows IT Support Specialists to track context from the original helpdesk message."}, {"step": 6, "action": "Submit the ticket. You will receive an email confirmation with your ticket ID (e.g., IT-4821). Monitor ticket status in Jira or watch for updates in the #it-helpdesk Slack channel."}, {"step": 7, "action": "When the issue is resolved, the assigned IT Support Specialist (e.g., Jordan Lee) will update the ticket status to 'Done' and add resolution notes. You will receive an automated email notification at that time."}], "ticket_lifecycle_statuses": ["Open", "In Progress", "Pending User", "Resolved", "Done"], "sla_targets": {"Critical": "First response within 30 minutes, resolution within 4 hours", "High": "First response within 2 hours, resolution within 1 business day", "Medium": "First response within 4 hours, resolution within 3 business days", "Low": "First response within 1 business day, resolution within 5 business days"}, "escalation_path": "If a ticket has not been updated within the SLA window, post in #it-helpdesk and tag the assigned specialist or the IT team lead.", "related_articles": ["KB-10447", "KB-10391"], "example_ticket_reference": "IT-4821", "status": "Published", "last_reviewed_by": "Jordan Lee", "last_reviewed_date": "2024-06-01", "created_date": "2022-09-08", "version": "2.3"}]}

mock_tools.description: Retrieve reference data from faq knowledge base data. Accepts queries containing a question or keywords and returns the most relevant FAQ entries including question, answer, and entry ID. When answering queries, call the `faq_knowledge_base_data` tool to retrieve data.
mock_tools.response_template:
{"faq_knowledge_base_data": {"entries": [{"entry_id": "KB-10447", "question": "Why does my VPN connection fail with error code 800 and how do I fix it?", "answer": "Error code 800 indicates that the VPN tunnel could not be established, typically due to a blocked port, misconfigured client, or server-side connectivity issue. Follow these steps to resolve it:

1. Confirm your internet connection is active and stable by visiting a public website.
2. Restart your VPN client completely (close from system tray, relaunch).
3. Check that UDP port 500 and 4500 are not blocked by your firewall or router — temporarily disable your local firewall to test.
4. Uninstall the VPN client, reboot your machine, then reinstall the latest version from the IT portal (https://itportal.internal/vpn).
5. Try switching VPN gateway servers — select a different region or server endpoint in the client settings.
6. If on a corporate network or hotel/office Wi-Fi, the network may block VPN protocols. Try using a mobile hotspot instead.
7. Flush your DNS cache: open Command Prompt as administrator and run `ipconfig /flushdns`.
8. If the issue persists after all steps, submit a ticket to the IT helpdesk via #it-helpdesk on Slack or email helpdesk@company.com and include your error code and device details.", "tags": ["vpn", "error code 800", "connectivity", "remote access", "network", "vpn client", "tunnel failure"], "category": "Network & Connectivity", "related_jira_ticket": "IT-4821", "affected_users_example": "Marcus Rivera", "slack_channel_reference": "#it-helpdesk", "thread_timestamp_reference": "1718200000.000100", "last_updated": "2024-06-12", "author": "Jordan Lee", "status": "Published"}, {"entry_id": "KB-10448", "question": "What should I do if I need urgent IT support before an important meeting or client call?", "answer": "If you have a time-sensitive IT issue before a meeting or client call, take the following steps immediately:

1. Post a message in the #it-helpdesk Slack channel with 🔴 at the start of your message to flag urgency. Include your name, the issue description, any error codes, and your deadline.
2. Tag @it-support in your Slack message to alert the on-call specialist.
3. An IT Support Specialist will triage your ticket and respond within 10 minutes for critical issues.
4. If Slack is unavailable, call the IT emergency line at ext. 5555.
5. As a temporary workaround for VPN issues, consider using a mobile hotspot or asking a colleague to share relevant files via cloud storage (SharePoint or Google Drive) for the duration of your call.
6. A Jira ticket will be automatically created or manually raised by the IT team with priority set to Critical to track resolution.
7. Once resolved, the ticket status will be updated to 'Done' and you will receive a Slack notification in the original thread.", "tags": ["urgent support", "it helpdesk", "client call", "meeting", "escalation", "critical priority", "slack helpdesk"], "category": "IT Support Process", "related_jira_ticket": "IT-4821", "affected_users_example": "Marcus Rivera", "slack_channel_reference": "#it-helpdesk", "thread_timestamp_reference": "1718200000.000100", "last_updated": "2024-06-12", "author": "Jordan Lee", "status": "Published"}, {"entry_id": "KB-10391", "question": "How are IT helpdesk Jira tickets prioritized and what does each status mean?", "answer": "IT helpdesk tickets are created in Jira under the IT project and follow a standard priority and status workflow:

Priority Levels:
- Critical: System down or issue blocking a business-critical activity (e.g., client call, production outage). Target response: 10 minutes. Example: IT-4821.
- High: Major functionality impaired but workaround exists. Target response: 1 hour.
- Medium: Partial functionality loss, non-urgent. Target response: 4 hours.
- Low: Cosmetic or minor issues. Target response: 1 business day.

Ticket Statuses:
- Open: Ticket received, awaiting assignment.
- In Progress: An IT specialist is actively working on the issue.
- Pending User: Awaiting information or action from the requester.
- Done: Issue resolved and verified. The ticket is closed.

Tickets are updated in real time by the assigned IT Support Specialist. When a ticket moves to 'Done', the requester receives an automated Slack notification in the original helpdesk thread. All tickets can be viewed at https://jira.internal/projects/IT.", "tags": ["jira", "ticket status", "priority", "it workflow", "done", "critical", "helpdesk process"], "category": "IT Support Process", "related_jira_ticket": "IT-4821", "last_updated": "2024-06-10", "author": "Jordan Lee", "status": "Published"}, {"entry_id": "KB-10312", "question": "How do I reinstall the corporate VPN client on Windows?", "answer": "To reinstall the corporate VPN client on a Windows laptop:

1. Open Control Panel > Programs > Uninstall a Program.
2. Locate the VPN client (e.g., Cisco AnyConnect, GlobalProtect, or your company's branded client) and click Uninstall. Follow the prompts.
3. Restart your computer after uninstallation completes.
4. Navigate to the IT portal at https://itportal.internal/vpn and download the latest installer for your operating system.
5. Run the installer as Administrator (right-click > Run as administrator).
6. Follow the installation wizard. When prompted, enter the VPN server address provided by IT: vpn.company.com.
7. Reboot your machine once installation is complete.
8. Launch the VPN client and attempt to connect using your corporate credentials (same as your Windows login).
9. If you still encounter error code 800 or other errors after reinstalling, contact the IT helpdesk via #it-helpdesk on Slack.", "tags": ["vpn", "reinstall", "vpn client", "windows", "cisco anyconnect", "globalprotect", "installation"], "category": "Network & Connectivity", "last_updated": "2024-05-28", "author": "IT Knowledge Base Team", "status": "Published"}, {"entry_id": "KB-10289", "question": "What information should I include when reporting an IT issue in the #it-helpdesk Slack channel?", "answer": "To help IT Support Specialists resolve your issue as quickly as possible, include the following information when posting in #it-helpdesk:

1. Urgency indicator: Use 🔴 for critical (blocking work), 🟡 for moderate, 🟢 for low priority.
2. Your full name and department.
3. Device type and operating system (e.g., Dell Latitude, Windows 11).
4. A clear description of the problem.
5. Any error codes or error messages you see (e.g., error code 800).
6. Steps you have already tried (e.g., restarted device, reinstalled client).
7. Your deadline or urgency context (e.g., 'client call in 30 minutes').
8. Screenshots or screen recordings if applicable (use Slack's file attachment feature).

Example post: '🔴 My laptop won't connect to the VPN and I have a client call in 30 minutes. I've tried restarting and reinstalling the client but it still fails with error code 800.'

The IT team will create a Jira ticket on your behalf and keep you updated in the thread.", "tags": ["slack", "it-helpdesk", "reporting", "issue template", "vpn", "error reporting", "best practices"], "category": "IT Support Process", "slack_channel_reference": "#it-helpdesk", "last_updated": "2024-06-01", "author": "Jordan Lee", "status": "Published"}, {"entry_id": "KB-10355", "question": "Why does VPN fail after a laptop restart and what are common causes?", "answer": "If your VPN fails to connect even after restarting your laptop, the following are common causes and resolutions:

1. Corrupted VPN client profile: Delete saved VPN profiles in the client settings and re-add the server address (vpn.company.com).
2. Network adapter conflict: Go to Device Manager > Network Adapters, right-click the VPN virtual adapter, and select 'Disable', then 'Enable'.
3. Windows update conflict: A recent Windows update may have altered network settings. Check Windows Update history and roll back if necessary, or contact IT.
4. Antivirus/firewall interference: Temporarily disable third-party antivirus software and attempt to reconnect. Re-enable immediately after testing.
5. Expired VPN certificate: Your device certificate may have expired. Contact IT to reissue — this cannot be self-resolved.
6. DNS resolution failure: Run `ipconfig /flushdns` and `ipconfig /registerdns` from an elevated Command Prompt.
7. Split tunneling misconfiguration: IT may need to adjust your VPN profile's split tunneling settings remotely.

If none of these resolve the issue, escalate via #it-helpdesk on Slack and reference your error code for faster triage.", "tags": ["vpn", "restart", "connection failure", "network adapter", "dns", "antivirus", "certificate", "error code 800"], "category": "Network & Connectivity", "last_updated": "2024-05-15", "author": "IT Knowledge Base Team", "status": "Published"}], "metadata": {"total_entries": 6, "knowledge_base_name": "IT Helpdesk FAQ Knowledge Base", "description": "Searchable repository of frequently asked questions, troubleshooting guides, and IT support process articles maintained by the IT Support team.", "primary_contact": "Jordan Lee", "contact_channel": "#it-helpdesk", "last_full_review": "2024-06-12"}}}
